frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment: Bhai honestly, in India you also have to be practical. If a girl is travelling alone late night or going to shady areas, obviously family will worry and ask questions like who was with her and why so late. It’s not about blaming anyone, but zamaana theek nahi hai, so women should be a little more careful about where they go and with whom.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women’s safety as their personal responsibility and normalizes questioning their behavior after harm instead of focusing on male violence. It encodes protective framing, victim questioning, and restriction advice disguised as practical concern.
original_frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
persona_text: You are Rohit. Age 28. Single. A software engineer in Pune. You are in a Reddit thread on r/India where someone posted about a man who has to pay 80000 rupees per month in alimony to his ex wife who has a job. You are commenting anonymously and sharing your opinion on whether you will ever get married given laws like this and what you think women's actual motivations for marriage are.
